title: cynoglossum nebrodense subsp. nebrodense
intro: Cynoglossum nebrodense subsp. nebrodense is a subspecies of the Cynoglossum nebrodense plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is also known as the "Mediterranean Hound's Tongue". The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Cynoglossum nebrodense subsp. nebrodense is often used as a tea or tincture to promote digestion, soothe sore throats, and alleviate cold and flu symptoms.
